mentions.note: Equity dilution risk similar to Alphabet and Amazon if capital needs force secondary offerings before SpaceX IPO closes.
May do a fundraiser per Cramer's speculation. Unknown if they'll need to raise capital for AI/infrastructure.

Heavy data center capex spending may force secondary offering; not yet communicated equity raise plans but competitive pressure vs. Google/Amazon may necessitate one. Expect selling pressure as Nvidia is liquidated.
Collaborating with Nvidia on ARM-based RTX Spark Superchip for AI agents; Microsoft ecosystem benefits from compute architecture shift. Gained 2% on Computex announcement.
Co-posting cryptic 'new era of PC' tweet with Nvidia; software giant pivoting to AI-native computing; magnificent May performance.

Value trap despite low enterprise value to sales multiple vs. Alphabet; owned 9 years, static valuation; opportunity cost vs. hardware winners horrendous; Ben Reitzes highlights vulnerability to non-voice AI, gaming exposure, Windows constraints, potential funnel for SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI capex.

Terrific cloud business but weighed down by enterprise software; Wall Street disdains AI product Copilot; Cramer's charitable trust still owns it because company must fix things with so much capital and talent; if they can't, stock will have to go (discussion tomorrow at noon CNBC Investing Club call).
Still important but no longer market leader; AI portion of business relevant, but traditional software segment facing secular headwinds from hardware/AI disruption.
Magnificent Seven member; secular growth in enterprise software and cloud; can handle rate hikes and recessions.
Led software rotation up 3% today; part of giant short squeeze alongside Salesforce, ServiceNow, Adobe; sustainability of move unclear.
Stock benefited from DDOG-led software rotation (+), lifting index despite mired-in-low-400s trading. Cramer considering profit-taking after parabolic move but retaining core position.
Even Microsoft (software play) caught in market's software aversion. Cramer says market hates software even though Microsoft is core.
Appears stuck with legacy software out of favor; Azure strong but no independent cloud valuation as subsidiary. Stock feels left behind despite cloud strength.
18% revenue growth, 23% EPS growth; Azure 40% growth solid; but overall guidance light, CapEx $190B forecast lacking clear ROI; stock down 4%.
Software business softness; no shortage story; software segment lacking punch. Could be considered cheap but without dividend, less attractive vs. Ford.
Reporting Q1 earnings tomorrow (earnings D-Day); Cramer owns in trust; serial underperformer (fell 10% day Jan 28, dragged market down); critical to watch data center capex and AI ROI messaging.
Reports Wednesday; four horsemen's health of data center buildout hinges on capex commentary. Critical earnings for AI narrative.
Can surprise with spectacular data center numbers; Copilot demand remains weak; holding in charitable trust.
First positive City research in months validates Azure strength and enterprise software resilience. Fantastic balance sheet can absorb AI competition hits. Stock up $6 to $424.
Software business not growing as desired; at risk from Anthropic and OpenAI but strong balance sheet enables major strategic pivots. Monitor for execution.
Down from $555 peak; lost OpenAI business to Amazon per weekend report; up 3.6% today on oversold bounce but down significantly YTD; AI net winner status questionable.
Trading poorly; too similar to SAP; both have ongoing problems; Cramer recommends swapping out to preserve capital; software weakness broad-based.
Software giant down slightly as hardware-AI trend dominates; despite strong fundamentals, facing sector headwinds and investor rotation.
Failed badly today despite captive Windows audience and forced Copilot adoption; palpable sense something's very wrong; Copilot needs redoing; on borrowed time.
One of greatest performers ever but despised now; can't catch bid until interest rates stop going higher; no love from institutions/individuals currently.
Enterprise software incumbents under siege from private AI upstarts; Microsoft is partner to OpenAI but losing pricing power as customers shift to AI-native platforms.
Azure hosts OpenAI; partnership with Nvidia for infrastructure; major beneficiary of CUDA ecosystem customer attraction.

Cramer very concerned about massive CAPEX spend on AI infrastructure; considering trimming or selling despite trust ownership. Major strategic pivot required; detailed concerns shared in club podcast.
Azure deceleration due to chip allocation to Copilot; weak Copilot adoption (15M paid users) disappointing; 45% bookings from OpenAI concerning; down 10%, now 24x earnings; needs better story.
Software provider caught in broad enterprise software selloff; down 3% on valuation compression despite strong fundamentals.
Least magnificent of Mag 7; convoluted earnings call confusing on AI strategy; OpenAI partnership status unclear; Co-Pilot only 15M paid users; Windows update fatigue real.

Down 10% after weak earnings/guidance; Copilot adoption (15M paid) only 1% of user base suggests monetization struggles. However, Nadella and Hood are smart—expect course correction. Buying levels emerging.
Beat on revenue and earnings but capex up 66% YoY (Azure AI investment); Wall Street worried about sustainability; stock sold off hard after hours despite beats.

Reports Wednesday. Watch CapEx guidance (no incremental increases desired) and Azure growth upside (39% vs. expected 36%). Copilot and Windows moat questions linger. Down 13% since last report; need reassurance on spending trajectory.
Weak partly due to false AI-related software pressure; P/E multiple compressed. Difficult to own except select days.
Magnificent 7 member; money temporarily flowing to storage plays but company has too much cash and smart leadership; patience needed.
Down almost 6% YTD as quality tech underperforms speculative names; fundamentals solid but rotation pressuring stock.
Data center energy costs pressuring stock; clean energy commitment hurting; needs cheaper fossil fuels or natural gas.
Down from $555 last summer due to AI spending concerns; rare pullback in quality name; buy weakness.

Up 15%, peaked at $555 in July then rolled over; Azure guidance light, capex growth reset higher disappointing; OpenAI debt concerns (27% stake, $250B Azure spend) easing if raises at $800B+ valuation; if Azure hits 39-40% growth, stock could roar; Cramer owns for CNBC Investing Club.
While Cramer likes Microsoft, he emphasizes the stock is far less attractive at $350 than at $250—valuation matters; higher prices make even great companies less desirable on risk-reward.
